//: nx.json vs project.json — what goes where:
nx.json targetDefaults: specifies behavior applied to a target NAME across ALL projects.
Use for: cache settings, input globs, and dependsOn patterns that are
identical for every package that has that target (e.g. every 'build'
project.json targets: everything specific to ONE project.
check:format, format, update:toc, and the ci aggregator target.
Per-package project.json files own: their own build, test, lint targets.
Rule: if a target name appears in only one project, it belongs in
project.json only, never in nx.json targetDefaults.
The ^ prefix in dependsOn (e.g. '^build') means: run the 'build' target
on all declared dependencies of this project first, not on the project itself.
Dependency relationships are declared via implicitDependencies in project.json
and via the dependencies field in package.json. NX combines both to resolve
the full build graph and guarantee correct execution order.
